recipes.title: Bhuna Khichuri
recipes.description: Rich rainy-day Bengali rice and lentil pilaf cooked dry with whole spices and ghee.
steps: Dry roast moong dal until lightly fragrant, then rinse with rice.
Heat ghee and bloom bay leaves and whole spices.
Add sliced onions and saute until lightly golden.
Stir in ginger and garlic pastes, turmeric, and cumin powder.
Add the rice and dal and toss to coat in the masala.
Pour in 1.2 liters of hot water with salt and slit chilies; bring to a boil.
Cover and cook on low for 18 minutes; rest 10 minutes before fluffing.

recipes.title: Hilsa Curry
recipes.description: Ilish fish simmered in mustard-onion gravy with green chilies, the celebrated Bengali national dish.
steps: Rub fish pieces with salt and turmeric and rest 10 minutes.
Heat mustard oil until smoking, then cool slightly.
Lightly fry the fish on both sides for 1 minute and remove.
In the same oil, saute onion paste, ginger, and garlic until oil separates.
Add turmeric, chili powder, and salt with a splash of water; cook into a thick masala.
Stir in mustard paste and 200 ml water and bring to a simmer.
Return fish to the pan, add slit green chilies, cover, and simmer 8 minutes.

recipes.title: Beef Bhuna
recipes.description: Slow-cooked, dark, intensely spiced beef curry that's reduced until the masala clings to every piece.
steps: Heat mustard oil and bloom whole spices.
Add sliced onions and fry until deep golden brown.
Add ginger and garlic pastes and cook 1 minute.
Whisk yogurt with bhuna masala and salt, then stir into the pot over low heat.
Add beef and toss to coat; cover and cook 1 hour stirring occasionally.
Uncover and bhuna (stir-fry on medium heat) until all moisture evaporates and oil rises.
Add a splash of water if needed to keep the meat tender; finish with a sprinkle of garam masala.

recipes.title: Mishti Doi
recipes.description: Bengali sweet yogurt set in earthen pots with caramelised sugar for a deep amber colour and silky texture.
steps: Reduce whole milk in a heavy pan to about two-thirds its volume, stirring often.
In a separate small pan, melt 100 g sugar to a deep caramel.
Carefully stir the caramel into the reduced milk; it will foam and dissolve.
Add remaining sugar and evaporated milk, then cool to lukewarm.
Whisk in the live yogurt as a starter culture.
Pour into earthen pots or ramekins.
Cover and rest in a warm spot for 8 hours to set, then refrigerate before serving.

recipes.title: Shorshe Ilish
recipes.description: Hilsa fish steamed in mustard paste, mustard oil, and green chilies — a defining Bengali dish.
steps: Soak both mustard seeds with 2 chilies and salt for 30 minutes.
Grind to a smooth paste with a little water.
Rub fish with turmeric and salt.
In a heatproof bowl, mix mustard paste with mustard oil, turmeric, and 100 ml water.
Add fish, coat well, and top with slit green chilies.
Cover and steam for 15 minutes until fish flakes and oil floats on top.
Drizzle a little raw mustard oil before serving with steamed rice.

recipes.title: Patishapta Pitha
recipes.description: Thin Bengali winter crepes filled with coconut, jaggery, and milk-based khoya.
steps: Whisk flours, semolina, milk, and a pinch of salt to a thin smooth batter; rest 1 hour.
Cook coconut and jaggery in a pan until thick and sticky for the kheer filling.
Stir in crumbled khoya and cook 2 more minutes; cool.
Heat a flat pan and brush with ghee.
Pour a thin layer of batter and tilt to spread into a small crepe.
Place a spoon of filling on one edge and roll up tightly while the crepe is still soft.
Repeat with remaining batter and serve warm or chilled.

recipes.title: Kacchi Biryani
recipes.description: Dhaka-style biryani with raw marinated mutton layered under parboiled rice and slow cooked in dum.
steps: Marinate mutton in yogurt, fried onions, ginger garlic paste, biryani masala, and salt for 2 hours.
Soak rice 30 minutes and parboil with whole spices and salt to 60 percent done.
Layer marinated raw mutton in a heavy pot with chunks of fried potato.
Pour parboiled rice over the meat, drizzle ghee, milk, and saffron.
Seal the pot tightly with dough or foil and a tight lid.
Cook on high heat 10 minutes, then on the lowest heat for 1.5 hours.
Rest 15 minutes off heat, then serve with borhani.

recipes.title: Aloo Bhorta
recipes.description: Bengali mashed potato relish with mustard oil, fried red chilies, onions, and coriander.
steps: Boil potatoes whole until fork tender, then peel.
Lightly toast dried chilies and garlic in a dry pan until fragrant.
In a mortar, pound chilies, garlic, and salt to a paste.
Mash potatoes coarsely with the spice paste.
Mix in finely chopped red onion and coriander leaves.
Drizzle mustard oil and adjust salt.
Serve at room temperature with steamed rice and dal.

recipes.title: Chingri Malai Curry
recipes.description: Bengali prawn curry simmered in spiced coconut milk with cinnamon, cardamom, and bay leaves.
steps: Marinate prawns with turmeric and salt for 10 minutes.
Heat ghee in a pan and lightly fry the prawns for 1 minute per side; remove.
Bloom whole spices in the same ghee.
Add onion paste and ginger and cook until oil separates.
Stir in chili powder and a splash of water to make a quick masala.
Pour in coconut milk and bring to a gentle simmer.
Return prawns to the pan and cook 5 minutes until just done; serve with rice.
